meta.description: Generate subtitle files (SRT, VTT) from narration text and optionally burn them into the video

step.name: extract_narration_text_and_timing
step.action: Extract narration text and timing data from the SSD. For each narration segment, collect: the spoken text content, start timestamp, end timestamp, and associated scene ID. Verify all narration segments have valid text and timing. Sort segments chronologically. Calculate total narration coverage as a percentage of video duration.
step.output: Ordered narration segments with text and timestamps

step.name: generate_srt
step.action: Generate an SRT (SubRip) subtitle file from the narration segments. Format each entry with: sequential index number, timestamp range (HH:MM:SS,mmm format), and subtitle text. Split long narration segments into multiple subtitle entries (maximum 2 lines, maximum 42 characters per line). Ensure no subtitle displays for longer than 7 seconds. Save to episodes/{episode_id}/exports/subtitles/{episode_id}.srt.

step.name: generate_vtt
step.action: Generate a WebVTT subtitle file from the same narration segments. Include the WEBVTT header. Format timestamps in HH:MM:SS.mmm format. Apply the same line-splitting rules as the SRT. Optionally include position and alignment cues for styled web playback. Save to episodes/{episode_id}/exports/subtitles/{episode_id}.vtt.

step.name: word_level_timing
step.action: If word-level timing is enabled in the SSD configuration, generate word-level subtitle variants. Break each narration segment into individual words with interpolated timing based on word length and speaking rate. Create word-level SRT and VTT files with the suffix "-words" (e.g., {episode_id}-words.srt). This enables karaoke-style highlighting in supported players.

step.name: optional_burn_in
step.action: If burn-in subtitles are requested in the configuration, overlay subtitles onto the video using FFmpeg. Use the subtitles filter with the generated SRT file: ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf "subtitles={srt-file}:force_style='FontSize=24,PrimaryColour=white,OutlineColour=black,Outline=2,Shadow=1,MarginV=40'" output.mp4. Configure font, size, color, outline, and position per the brand style guide. Save the burned-in version alongside the clean version.

quality-gates.gate: All narration segments extracted with valid text and timing
SRT file generated with correct formatting and sequential indices
VTT file generated with WEBVTT header and correct formatting
Subtitle lines do not exceed 42 characters or 2 lines per entry
No subtitle displays for longer than 7 seconds
Word-level timing files generated if enabled
Burn-in subtitles rendered correctly if requested
Subtitle timing aligns with narration audio
